name: Ohio (United States)

colors.symbolism: evokes the red of the U.S. flag and the hills and valleys of the state
evokes the roads and waterways of the state
the hills and valleys of the state

description: A swallow-tailed burgee. A blue isoceles triangle at the hoist (its base on the hoist edge, apex at the centre of the flag) bears 17 white five-pointed stars arranged in two arcs around a white annulus enclosing a red disc. To the fly, three red and two white horizontal stripes alternate, with the fly cut into two pointed tails by a triangular notch.
history: Designed by architect John Eisenmann for Ohio's pavilion at the 1901 Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo and adopted by the Ohio General Assembly on 9 May 1902. It is the only non-rectangular U.S. state flag. The swallowtail shape derives from the cavalry guidons of the U.S. Army.

features.role: blue triangular field at the hoist
outer arc of stars on the hoist triangle
features.symbolism: the original thirteen colonies of the United States

features.role: inner arc of stars on the hoist triangle
features.symbolism: together with the thirteen, total seventeen — Ohio's place as the 17th state admitted to the Union
represents the letter O for Ohio and the buckeye nut, the state tree's seed

features.role: red disc inside the white annulus
